design_philosophy.core_loop: Match cookies in rows/columns by rotating grid lines like a Rubik's Cube; clear all cookies before grid overflow from top and right sides
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Spatial puzzle solving under time pressure as grid fills; in VS mode, player actions directly disrupt opponent's board
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Satisfying tile-matching puzzle mastery with Yoshi branding; competitive disruption in multiplayer
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rotation-based matching mechanic (rather than swapping) creates unique spatial reasoning demands; strong emphasis on competitive VS mode where players can actively sabotage opponents

difficulty.checkpointing: Successive levels in Action Mode progressively grow more complex
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Puzzle Mode offers predefined grids with move limits, allowing players to tackle specific puzzle constraints; VS mode difficulty scales with AI opponent quality

progression.unlock_mechanism: Successive levels unlock in Action Mode; Puzzle Mode provides discrete puzzle challenges with move limits
replayability_hooks: Competitive VS Mode (single-player vs AI, or two-player split-screen)
Puzzle Mode with defined grids and move constraints
Grid grows in complexity across levels in Action Mode
Yoshi wild card mechanic adds variability to each run

world_building_techniques: Yoshi branding and character theming (Yoshi head as wild card)
Mario universe licensing applied to pure puzzle game
Unused cutscenes and character graphics suggest developmental narrative intent later abandoned

faction_indicators: VS Mode split-screen competition directly pits players against each other
Player actions in multiplayer cause direct disruptions to opponent board
danger_signals: Grid overflow as universal loss condition
Opponent's strategic board manipulation in VS mode as immediate threat
environmental_storytelling: Cookie grid visual presentation tied to Yoshi/Mario universe
Grid growth from top and right creates spatial pressure narrative

sources_used: Wikipedia - Yoshi's Cookie (gameplay, development, release, designer credits)
The Cutting Room Floor - Yoshi's Cookie (SNES) (unused assets, regional differences, development context)
